name: Microsoft Cloud Platform Enterprise Architecture Ontology
description: Microsoft-specific cloud platform ontology covering Azure resources, Power Platform, Microsoft 365, Azure Well-Architected Framework (WAF) pillars, and Azure AI service bindings. Extends EA-CORE-ONT with Microsoft cloud implementation details and imports EA-AI-ONT for vendor-neutral AI/ML entities. Designed for future parallel extensions (EA-AWS-ONT, EA-GCP-ONT).

oaa:imports.name: EA-CORE-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Platform-agnostic EA core providing Application, TechnologyArchitecture, SecurityArchitecture, and BusinessCapability base types

oaa:imports.name: EA-AI-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Vendor-neutral AI/ML architecture ontology providing AIWorkload, AIAgent, RAGSystem, and ResponsibleAIControl base types for Azure AI service bindings

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Application-to-Azure Hosting
oaa:joinPatterns.description: EA-CORE applications are hosted on Microsoft cloud platform resources

oaa:joinPatterns.name: WAF Assessment Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: EA-CORE applications are assessed against WAF pillars for architecture quality

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Azure AI Service Binding
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Vendor-neutral AI workloads are bound to specific Azure AI services for deployment
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Azure AI workload deployment tracking and cost management

entities.name: CloudPlatform
entities.description: A cloud provider platform (Microsoft Azure, future: AWS, GCP) that hosts enterprise applications and services. CloudPlatform is the root anchor for platform-specific resource hierarchies.

entities.name: AzureResource
entities.description: Base class for all Azure cloud resources, representing any Azure Resource Manager (ARM) managed entity. Provides common Azure properties including resource ID, region, SKU tier, and tags.

oaa:properties.name: skuTier
oaa:properties.description: Azure service tier (Free, Basic, Standard, Premium)

oaa:properties.name: tags
oaa:properties.description: Azure resource tags for governance and cost tracking

entities.name: AzureSubscription
entities.description: Azure billing and access control boundary that contains resource groups and resources. Subscriptions are the primary unit of Azure cost management and RBAC scope.

entities.name: AzureResourceGroup
entities.description: Logical container for Azure resources that share the same lifecycle, permissions, and policies. Resource groups are deployed to a specific Azure region.

entities.name: AzureLandingZone
entities.description: Pre-configured Azure environment following Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) best practices with identity, networking, governance, and security foundations in place.

entities.name: PowerPlatform
entities.description: Microsoft Power Platform low-code application development environment including Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, and Power Pages for citizen developer and professional developer scenarios.

entities.name: Microsoft365
entities.description: Microsoft 365 productivity and collaboration suite including Teams, SharePoint, Exchange, OneDrive, and associated services providing the digital workplace foundation.

entities.name: WAFPillar
entities.description: A foundational pillar of the Azure Well-Architected Framework. The five pillars (Reliability, Security, Cost Optimization, Operational Excellence, Performance Efficiency) provide a structured approach to architecture quality assessment.

oaa:properties.name: azureServices
oaa:properties.description: Key Azure services supporting this pillar

entities.name: DesignPrinciple
entities.description: A guiding principle from the Azure Well-Architected Framework that informs architecture decisions within a specific pillar (e.g., Design for Failure, Defense in Depth, Zero Trust, Infrastructure as Code).

entities.name: AssessmentCriterion
entities.description: A measurable criterion for architecture assessment against WAF pillars with defined units of measurement (e.g., RTO in minutes, Secure Score as percentage, P95 Latency in milliseconds).

oaa:properties.name: unitCode
oaa:properties.description: Unit of measurement (MIN, HUR, P1, SEC, DAY)

entities.name: AzureAIService
entities.description: An Azure AI service resource providing AI/ML capabilities (Azure OpenAI, Azure AI Search, Azure ML, Azure AI Studio, Azure AI Content Safety). Binds vendor-neutral ea-ai:AIWorkload entities to Azure-specific deployments with resource IDs, endpoints, and SKUs.

oaa:properties.name: modelDeployment
oaa:properties.description: Model deployment name within the Azure AI service

relationships.name: hostedOnPlatform
relationships.description: An EA-CORE application is hosted on a cloud platform, establishing the runtime environment for cross-platform portfolio analysis

relationships.name: belongsToSubscription
relationships.description: An Azure resource belongs to exactly one Azure subscription for billing and access control governance

relationships.name: containsResource
relationships.description: An Azure resource group contains one or more Azure resources that share the same lifecycle and governance policies

relationships.name: assessedByPillar
relationships.description: An EA-CORE application is assessed against WAF pillars for architecture quality scoring and improvement planning

relationships.name: belongsToPillar
relationships.description: A WAF design principle belongs to exactly one WAF pillar, establishing the principle-to-pillar taxonomy

relationships.name: measuresPillar
relationships.description: An assessment criterion measures a specific WAF pillar, providing quantifiable metrics for architecture quality

relationships.name: providedByPlatform
relationships.description: An Azure resource is provided by the Microsoft cloud platform, anchoring all Azure resources to the platform root

relationships.name: governedByLandingZone
relationships.description: An Azure subscription is governed by a landing zone providing pre-configured governance, networking, and security foundations

relationships.name: deployedOnAzureAI
relationships.description: A vendor-neutral AI workload is deployed on a specific Azure AI service, binding abstract AI architecture to Azure infrastructure

enumerations.name: CloudProvider
enumerations.description: Cloud platform providers. Microsoft is implemented; AWS and GCP reserved for future EA-AWS-ONT and EA-GCP-ONT extensions.

enumerations.name: WAFPillarName
enumerations.description: The five pillars of the Azure Well-Architected Framework

hasDefinedTerm.name: Reliability
hasDefinedTerm.description: System recovery from failures and continued function

hasDefinedTerm.name: Security
hasDefinedTerm.description: Protect applications and data from threats

hasDefinedTerm.name: CostOptimization
hasDefinedTerm.description: Manage costs to maximise delivered value

hasDefinedTerm.name: PerformanceEfficiency
hasDefinedTerm.description: System ability to adapt to load changes

enumerations.name: AzureAIServiceType
enumerations.description: Types of Azure AI services for AI workload deployment

hasDefinedTerm.name: AzureOpenAI
hasDefinedTerm.description: Azure OpenAI Service for GPT, DALL-E, and Whisper models

hasDefinedTerm.name: AzureAISearch
hasDefinedTerm.description: Azure AI Search for vector and hybrid search

hasDefinedTerm.name: AzureML
hasDefinedTerm.description: Azure Machine Learning for model training and deployment

hasDefinedTerm.name: AzureAIStudio
hasDefinedTerm.description: Azure AI Studio for prompt engineering and evaluation

hasDefinedTerm.name: AzureContentSafety
hasDefinedTerm.description: Azure AI Content Safety for responsible AI controls

hasDefinedTerm.name: AzureDocumentIntelligence
hasDefinedTerm.description: Azure AI Document Intelligence for document processing

businessRules.name: AzureResourceSubscriptionMembership
businessRules.description: Every Azure resource must belong to exactly one subscription for billing and governance
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one ea-msft:belongsToSubscription relationship to an ea-msft:AzureSubscription

businessRules.name: WAFPillarCompleteness
businessRules.description: Each WAF pillar must have at least one design principle and one assessment criterion for actionable guidance
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST be referenced by at least one ea-msft:DesignPrinciple via belongsToPillar AND one ea-msft:AssessmentCriterion via measuresPillar

businessRules.name: LandingZoneSubscriptionGovernance
businessRules.description: Landing zones must govern at least one subscription to justify their configuration
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST be referenced by at least one ea-msft:AzureSubscription via ea-msft:governedByLandingZone

businessRules.name: DesignPrinciplePillarAssignment
businessRules.description: Design principles must belong to exactly one WAF pillar for clear categorisation
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one ea-msft:belongsToPillar relationship to an ea-msft:WAFPillar

businessRules.name: MicrosoftPlatformWAFAssessment
businessRules.description: Microsoft cloud platforms should have WAF assessment coverage for architecture quality governance
businessRules.action: THEN applications hosted on it SHOULD be assessed against at least one ea-msft:WAFPillar

businessRules.name: AzureAIServiceEndpoint
businessRules.description: Azure AI services must specify an endpoint for connectivity
businessRules.action: THEN it SHOULD have a non-empty endpoint property

changeControl.changeNotes: v1.1.0: AI entity extraction to EA-AI-ONT. Removed 5 AI entities (AIWorkload, AIAgent, RAGSystem, AIArchitecturePattern, ResponsibleAIControl), 4 AI relationships (usesModel, hasTools, implementsPattern, securedByControl), 2 AI enumerations (AIPatternType, ResponsibleAICategory), 3 AI business rules (BR-MSFT-003/004/005). Added EA-AI-ONT import. Added AzureAIService entity and deployedOnAzureAI relationship for Azure-specific AI service bindings. Added AzureAIServiceType enumeration. 11 entities, 9 relationships, 6 business rules, 3 enumerations.

versionHistory.notes: Initial release: Microsoft cloud platform ontology from V3 source consolidation
AI entity extraction to EA-AI-ONT. Added EA-AI import, AzureAIService entity, deployedOnAzureAI relationship. Phase 2 of EA Sub-Series Restructure.
